page: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The system features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 154 GB of temporary storage capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile characterized by average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores are in the average range, as is single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register as weak, although the overall PassMark memory score remains average.

From a resource density perspective, the E4ds_v4 offers a high memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. Tradeoffs include lower multi-threaded efficiency, as evidenced by poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and weak database operations. This server is suited for relational database caching, memory-intensive web applications, and lightweight data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw multi-core compute throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ds_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, it features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and includes 154 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, contrasted by weaker multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high parallel compute scaling. Tradeoffs include lower multi-threaded throughput and weak database operation metrics. This instance is suited for memory-bound applications, caching layers, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and bundled local storage for memory-bound workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
154 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability

Suited for memory-intensive caching, databases, and moderate web serving
